response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work), strong systems-level background (low-latency DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and caching), and demonstrated Linux/server expertise. However, modern C++ experience is dated (National Instruments, 2014-2015) and there is no Rust, and no direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience. Recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node full-stack, so the fit for a C++-primary routing data pipeline role is partial and would require an interview to probe systems depth and C++ currency.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience as founder of an open-source GIS project used in production
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) in a performance-oriented language (DLang)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and cross-stack adaptability
concerns: Commercial C++ experience is roughly a decade old and no Rust experience is listed
No direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline background
Recent roles are TypeScript/Node product work, distant from systems-level pipeline engineering
